title: Use zebra striping only when it improves scanability
summary: AI-generated tables apply alternating row colors to every table by default, even when the table has few rows or clear column separation.
instruction: Use zebra striping (alternating row background colors) only when the table has enough rows and columns that tracking across the table is difficult without it. Small tables with clear column separation do not benefit from alternating backgrounds.
rationale: Zebra striping adds visual noise that can counteract its intended benefit when few rows or well-separated columns already make tracking easy. The decision should be based on whether the table's dimensions and density genuinely require the extra tracking aid, not on a reflex to stripe every table.

violations: A 4-row settings table with alternating row colors where columns are clearly separated.
A 6-row data table with 3 columns using zebra striping when row hover would suffice.
controls: A 50-row data table with 8 columns where zebra striping helps track across wide content.
A 4-row table with no zebra striping but 12px row padding and clear column headers.
borderline: A calendar grid where alternating row colors help distinguish weeks — the entire row is a temporal unit.

exceptions.description: Design systems that apply zebra striping consistently for brand style. Accessibility overrides where users prefer high-contrast row separation.

detector.signalDescription: Detect nth-child background color CSS rules on table rows. Compare row count (from HTML or runtime) against column count. Flag small tables where zebra striping is likely unnecessary.
limitations: Row count may be dynamically generated and not present in static HTML.
CSS class-based striping (odd/even classes) is harder to detect than nth-child rules.
Responsive tables may hide columns, changing the width justification.

scenarios: Design systems that apply zebra striping to all tables intentionally.
Accessibility settings that add high-contrast row differentiation.
mitigations: Auto-detect row count from HTML when available. Allow suppression for design-system-level defaults.

fixturePlan.description: Same 5-row table with no zebra striping, just row hover highlight.
A 12-row, 9-column table with zebra striping justified by table dimensions.
